hostsDialog.host.info: By default, if a request host is set in the Xray config, this host is used. However, you can set a custom request host here if needed.
hostsDialog.host.multiHost: To set multiple addresses, separate them with <badge>,</badge> Each time an address is chosen randomly.
hostsDialog.host.wildcard: Use <badge>*</badge> to generate a random string (works for wildcard domain names)
hostsDialog.jalaliExpireDate: Expiry date of the user in solar calendar
hostsDialog.noise.info.attention: Attention: currently, this feature only supported in streisand >= 1.6.32 and v2rayNG >= 1.8.39 (custom config)
hostsDialog.path.info: Set a path for host users, useful behind a reverse proxy.
hostsDialog.port.info: By default, a host uses the default port of the inbound. You can set a custom port in case this host is a server that forwards traffic from a port that differs from your server's port. For example, the server may forward traffic from port 8443 to the default port of your inbound server.
hostsDialog.proxyOutbound.info: Extra outbound ( only in v2ray custom config )
hostsDialog.security.info: If the middleware server of this host uses a different security layer than the inbound's default, you can set a custom security layer here.
hostsDialog.sni.info: By default, a host uses the default SNI of the inbound. You can set a custom SNI in case this host is a server that has a different SNI. For example, the server may receive traffic with a different SSL certificate, perform SSL termination, and forward it to your inbound server.
hostsDialog.title: Using this setting, you are able to assign specific address for each inbound.

hostsPage.noInbounds: No inbound is available. Configure an inbound before adding hosts.

inbounds.accounts.label: Account
inbounds.portWarningBlocked: This port is commonly blocked by servers/firewalls. Better to avoid it.
inbounds.http.noAccountsHint: Add at least one username/password pair.
inbounds.l2tp.help.accounting: Record L2TP/IPsec session traffic and report it to the same Rebecca user quota/accounting pipeline.
inbounds.l2tp.help.dns: DNS resolvers pushed to L2TP clients, one IPv4 address per line.
inbounds.l2tp.help.ipsecPsk: Shared IPsec secret used by clients before L2TP username/password authentication.
inbounds.l2tp.help.ipv4Pool: Private IPv4 range assigned to L2TP users. Each user receives a deterministic address from this pool.
inbounds.l2tp.help.lcpEchoFailure: How many missed PPP keepalive probes are allowed before the L2TP session is considered dead.
inbounds.l2tp.help.lcpEchoInterval: Seconds between PPP keepalive probes used to detect dead L2TP sessions.
inbounds.l2tp.help.mru: PPP MRU for L2TP clients. Keep it close to MTU unless you have a path-specific reason.
inbounds.l2tp.help.mtu: PPP MTU for L2TP clients. 1410 is a conservative default for IPsec/NAT paths.
inbounds.l2tp.help.redirectGateway: Route all client traffic through the L2TP/IPsec VPN.
inbounds.l2tp.help.tproxy: Forward VPN client traffic into Xray so routing rules and Xray outbounds apply. Disable for direct NAT egress from the node.
inbounds.l2tp.help.tunnelPort: Internal Xray tunnel port used by nftables/TProxy. It must be unique and different from the public L2TP port.
inbounds.openvpn.help.accounting: Record OpenVPN session traffic and report it to the same Rebecca user quota/accounting pipeline.
inbounds.openvpn.help.auth: Optional packet authentication digest such as SHA256. Leave empty to use OpenVPN defaults.
inbounds.openvpn.help.authNoCache: Add auth-nocache so the client does not keep the VPN password in memory after authentication.
inbounds.openvpn.help.blockOutsideDns: Add block-outside-dns for Windows clients to reduce DNS leaks outside the VPN.
inbounds.openvpn.help.ca: Certificate authority used to sign the OpenVPN server certificate. A self-signed CA is fine for personal use.
inbounds.openvpn.help.cipher: Optional OpenVPN data cipher. Leave empty to use the OpenVPN default for your installed version.
inbounds.openvpn.help.dh: Optional Diffie-Hellman parameters for older TLS modes. Usually not needed with modern ECDHE/OpenVPN setups.
inbounds.openvpn.help.dns: DNS resolvers pushed to OpenVPN clients, one IPv4 address per line.
inbounds.openvpn.help.embedCredentials: Include username and generated password inside the .ovpn profile. Disable to make clients ask for credentials.
inbounds.openvpn.help.extraClient: Extra directives appended to generated client profiles. Use only valid OpenVPN client options.
inbounds.openvpn.help.inlineCa: Include the CA certificate inside generated .ovpn files. Disable only if clients will receive the CA by another secure method.
inbounds.openvpn.help.ipv4Pool: Private IPv4 range assigned to OpenVPN users. Each user receives a deterministic address from this pool.
inbounds.openvpn.help.managementPort: Optional local OpenVPN management port used by the node process. Leave empty unless you need explicit control.
inbounds.openvpn.help.redirectGateway: Push the default route to clients so all client traffic enters the VPN.
inbounds.openvpn.help.routeNoPull: Add route-nopull so clients connect but ignore routes pushed by the server.
inbounds.openvpn.help.serverCertificate: OpenVPN server certificate signed by the CA above. Clients verify the server with this trust chain.
inbounds.openvpn.help.serverKey: Private key for the OpenVPN server certificate. Keep it private; it is written only to the node's OpenVPN config.
inbounds.openvpn.help.setClientCertNone: Add setenv CLIENT_CERT 0 so OpenVPN Connect does not ask for a separate client certificate.
inbounds.openvpn.help.tlsAuth: Optional static HMAC key for authenticating the OpenVPN control channel.
inbounds.openvpn.help.tlsCrypt: Optional static key that encrypts and authenticates the OpenVPN control channel.
inbounds.openvpn.help.requireDco: Require OpenVPN data channel offload on the node. If the kernel or OpenVPN build cannot use DCO, this inbound is rejected instead of falling back.
inbounds.openvpn.help.tproxy: Forward OpenVPN client traffic into Xray so routing rules and Xray outbounds apply. Disable for direct NAT egress from the node.
inbounds.openvpn.help.transport: Select UDP for the usual OpenVPN mode, or TCP when UDP is blocked by the network.
inbounds.openvpn.help.tunnelPort: Internal Xray tunnel port used by nftables/TProxy. It must be unique and different from the public OpenVPN port.
inbounds.wireguard.generateKeysError: Unable to generate WireGuard key pair
inbounds.wireguard.help.accounting: Record WireGuard peer traffic and report it to the same Rebecca user quota/accounting pipeline.
inbounds.wireguard.help.ipv4Pool: Private IPv4 range assigned to WireGuard users. Each user receives a deterministic address from this pool.
inbounds.wireguard.help.mtu: WireGuard interface MTU. 1420 is the common default; lower it only for path-specific fragmentation issues.
inbounds.wireguard.help.nat: Allow direct NAT egress from the WireGuard interface. Keep it off when traffic must pass through Xray routing rules.
inbounds.wireguard.help.persistentKeepal: Seconds between client keepalive packets. 25 helps clients behind NAT stay reachable; 0 disables it.
inbounds.wireguard.help.privateKey: Private key used by the WireGuard server interface. Generate a key pair here unless you already have one.
inbounds.wireguard.help.publicKey: Server public key shown for reference and future profile generation. The node derives the runtime key from the private key.
inbounds.wireguard.help.serverAddress: WireGuard address assigned to the server interface. Keep it inside the selected IPv4 pool.
inbounds.wireguard.help.tproxy: Forward WireGuard client traffic into Xray so routing rules and Xray outbounds apply. Disable for direct NAT egress from the node.
inbounds.wireguard.help.tunnelPort: Internal Xray tunnel port used by nftables/TProxy. It must be unique and different from the public WireGuard port.
inbounds.sniffingHint: It is recommended to keep the default.
inbounds.socks.noAccountsHint: Add at least one account for password mode.

userDialog.ipLimitHint: Maximum unique IPs allowed per user. Leave empty or '-' for unlimited.

userDialog.serviceManagedNotice: This user is tied to service {{service}}. Update the service to change shared settings.
userDialog.statusNoticeLimited: Data limit reached; reset or increase the quota to reconnect.
userDialog.statusNoticeExpired: This user's time has expired. Add more time to restore access.
userDialog.statusNoticeOnHold: This user is on hold; the timer starts on first connection.
userDialog.statusNoticeDisabled: This user is disabled. Enable the switch next to the username field.
userDialog.brokenServiceWarning: Selected service has no hosts. Please pick another service.
userDialog.trafficDepletedWarning: This user's traffic is fully used and they can no longer connect.
userDialog.expiredWarning: This user's subscription has expired and they can no longer connect.
userDialog.onHoldWarning: This user is on hold; once they connect for the first time, their status will switch to active.
userDialog.serviceModeHint: The selected service controls protocol and host settings.

userDialog.usersLimitReached: User limit reached. You have {{active}} active users out of {{limit}} allowed.

userDialog.unlimitedNotAllowed: Unlimited data is not allowed. Maximum allowed: {{max}} GB
userDialog.createdTrafficUnlimitedNotAll: Unlimited data is not allowed while your admin traffic mode is created traffic.
userDialog.dataLimitBelowUsedTraffic: Data limit cannot be lower than this user's used traffic ({{used}}).
